subject: Set internal name per portal team

Patch Set 1:

Lorraine, will this be OK? I verified that this name resolves, and I think it's better for HTTPS (single token is not verifiable).  
root@aai-resources:/opt/app/aai-resources# ping portal-app.onap
PING portal-app.onap.svc.cluster.local (10.43.148.139) 56(84) bytes of data.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Just asked the Portal developer and he said No they do not equate

Patch Set 1:

We can do it that way if you really insist, but it seems like bad practice to hard code a non-fully qualified domain name for which certificate issuers are disallowed from issuing a certificate. See https://www.entrustdatacard.com/blog/2013/february/ssl-certificates-without-non-fqdns
"the publicly-trusted SSL CAs will stop issuing certificates with non-FQDNs by November 1, 2015, and all unexpired certificates with non-FQDNs will be revoked by October 1, 2016. The CAs must also provide a warning of the deprecated use of such certificate to the applicant before issuance."
The names are functionally equivalent based on the view from AAI in the lab.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

I will relay your concern back to the team. Thank you
